mappings.rationale: Directly covers all three laws of thermodynamics, entropy, Carnot cycle, Maxwell's equations, and thermodynamic relations central to this section.
Covers gas laws, solutions, phase changes, colligative properties, and the thermodynamic aspects of chemical systems discussed in this section.
Covers states of matter, solutions, gas behaviour, and thermodynamic concepts relevant to the chemical aspects of this section.
Directly covers the gaseous, liquid, and plasma states of matter, phase transitions, and the properties of matter at extreme conditions.
Covers colloids, polymers in solution, surface phenomena, and the rheological properties of complex fluids discussed in this section.
Directly covers fluid statics, hydrodynamics, laminar and turbulent flow, viscosity, and transport phenomena discussed in the section.
Covers the liquid state properties of water, solutions, surface tension, and the behaviour of water in different thermodynamic conditions.
Directly relevant to the section's coverage of special phenomena at very low temperatures including superconductivity and superfluidity.
Covers heat, thermodynamics, states of matter, and the fundamental principles of thermal physics discussed in this section.
Covers the historical development of thermodynamics, kinetic theory, and the theories of heat discussed in this section.
Relevant to statistical thermodynamics and the statistical-mechanical treatment of physical systems discussed in this section.
Relevant to the properties of matter at extreme conditions, superconductivity, superfluidity, and quantum effects at low temperatures.
Relevant to molecular interactions in solutions, colloids, and the relationship between molecular properties and bulk matter behaviour.
Relevant to surface phenomena, adsorption, and colloid science discussed in the section's coverage of physical effects at surfaces.
Relevant to heat transfer, thermal energy applications, and plasma applications for power production discussed in this section.
Relevant to the effects of low temperature on magnetic properties and the behaviour of plasmas in magnetic fields discussed in this section.
Relevant to the thermal and mechanical properties of materials, including effects of temperature and pressure on material properties.
Relevant to nonlinear dynamical behaviour in fluid systems and complex transport phenomena discussed in this section.
Relevant to the plasma state and thermonuclear fusion conditions, including plasma formation and confinement discussed in this section.
Relevant to methods of measuring thermodynamic quantities, temperature scales, and characterizing low-temperature states discussed in this section.
Covers foundational concepts in statistical mechanics and the philosophical underpinnings of thermodynamic laws discussed in this section.
Relevant to crystal growth from solutions and melts, phase equilibria, and the transition between liquid and solid states.
Covers entropy, thermodynamics, the Carnot cycle, and energy transitions — core topics of this section on heat and thermodynamics.
